settings.question: How do I know if my wheels need alignment?
settings.answer: Common signs of misalignment include: your vehicle pulling to the left or right on a straight road, uneven or rapid tire wear (more wear on inside or outside edge), a steering wheel that's off-centre when driving straight, or a vibrating steering wheel. If you notice any of these, come in for an alignment check — catching it early prevents premature tire wear.

settings.question: How often should I get a wheel alignment?
settings.answer: We recommend checking alignment annually or every 20,000 km, and always after hitting a major pothole, curb, or in any collision. Calgary's roads are tough on alignment — freeze-thaw cycles create significant potholes every spring. It's also good practice to check alignment when installing a new set of tires to maximize tread life.

settings.question: How does misalignment affect my tires?
settings.answer: Even slight misalignment causes uneven tread wear — you may notice one edge of your tire wearing down much faster than the rest. In severe cases, a set of tires that should last 80,000 km can wear out in 30,000 km. A single alignment service typically costs far less than replacing tires prematurely.

settings.question: Will alignment fix my car pulling to one side?
settings.answer: In most cases, yes. A vehicle that pulls to one side is a classic alignment symptom, and a computerized alignment will correct it. Occasionally pulling can also be caused by uneven tire inflation or a worn suspension component — our technicians will inspect and identify the root cause before we begin.

settings.question: Can I get alignment done when I get new tires?
settings.answer: Absolutely — and we recommend it. Installing new tires on a misaligned vehicle means your brand-new tires start wearing unevenly from day one. Combining alignment with a tire installation is the best way to protect your investment and get the most life out of your new tires.

settings.heading: Wheel Alignment Calgary
settings.subheading: Extend your tire life and restore your vehicle's handling. Computerized wheel alignment at Prince Tires — precise, fast, and done right the first time.
settings.pricing_intro: Our computerized alignment system measures and corrects all four wheels to manufacturer specifications. Most vehicles are done in under an hour. We'll show you the before-and-after printout so you can see exactly what was adjusted.
